issue.title: Bug: /tiki:release can't create release JSON — desktop UI was the only writer, leaving v0.4.1+ without JSON on disk

successCriteria.description: /tiki:release creates .tiki/releases/{version}.json from a minimal scaffold when the file does not already exist (instead of erroring)
Dependency Graph release dropdown surfaces archived releases (releases/archive/*.json), not just top-level releases/*.json
v0.4.1, v0.4.2, v0.5.0 release JSON files exist in .tiki/releases/archive/ with valid TikiRelease shape (parse cleanly via load_tiki_releases, no warn logs)
Startup or load-time warning logs a discrepancy when changelog count exceeds JSON count under .tiki/releases/ (top-level + archive/)
.claude/commands/tiki/audit.md is byte-identical to packages/framework/commands/audit.md (the missing <errors> and <next-actions> sections are restored)
Rust changes compile cleanly via cargo check; no new clippy warnings on touched code paths
Existing sidebar ReleasesSection still works (does not see archived releases by default — preserves the #142 fix that hid shipped releases from the sidebar)

phases.title: Add create-if-absent step to release.md (framework prose)
phases.content:
Update step 2 of `<instructions>` in BOTH `.claude/commands/tiki/release.md` AND `packages/framework/commands/release.md` (they're mirrors — both must change).

Current step 2: 'Load release definition from .tiki/releases/{version}.json — Validate the file exists'.

New step 2 logic:
1. If `.tiki/releases/{version}.json` exists: load it (current behavior).
2. If it does NOT exist: write a minimal scaffold via the Write tool (the framework cannot call save_tiki_release — that's a Tauri IPC, desktop-only).

Scaffold shape:
```json
{
  "version": "{version}",
  "status": "active",
  "issues": [ /* derived from --issues flag, gh milestone query, or interactive prompt */ ],
  "createdAt": "{ISO}"
}
```

The issue-list derivation order:
a. `--issues '147,148,149'` CLI flag (preferred).
b. GitHub milestone matching the version (gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/milestones?state=open).
c. Interactive prompt asking for issue numbers (via AskUserQuestion).

Update `<error type="release-not-found">` to reflect that auto-create is now the default; the error becomes the fallback when no issue list can be derived at all.

This phase touches prose only — no Rust, no TS. Verification via Read tool to confirm the edits landed in both files.
verification: `.claude/commands/tiki/release.md` step 2 instructions describe create-if-absent behavior
`packages/framework/commands/release.md` step 2 instructions are byte-identical to the .claude/ copy
`<error type="release-not-found">` reworded as fallback case rather than primary error
git diff shows both files updated, no other prose files touched in this phase

phases.title: Expose archived releases via load_tiki_releases includeArchived flag
phases.content:
Extend `load_tiki_releases` (`apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:216`) to optionally include files from `releases/archive/`.

Signature change:
```rust
pub fn load_tiki_releases(
    tiki_path: Option<String>,
    include_archived: Option<bool>,  // default false
) -> Result<Vec<TikiRelease>, String>
```

Implementation:
- Existing logic stays for top-level `releases/*.json`.
- If `include_archived.unwrap_or(false)`, also scan `releases/archive/*.json` and append to the result. Same parse + warn-on-fail behavior as the existing loop.
- Final sort via `cmp_semver` still applies to the merged list.

Frontend wiring:
- `apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/useDependencyGraph.ts` — when loading releases for the graph, pass `includeArchived: true` via `invoke('load_tiki_releases', { tikiPath, includeArchived: true })`. NOTE: the current code path goes through `App.tsx:290`'s shared loader. Cleanest fix: add a new effect in `useDependencyGraph` that does its own `invoke` with `includeArchived: true` and populates a local-to-the-hook list, OR thread the flag through the store. Prefer the latter only if multiple components need archived releases.
- Sidebar `ReleasesSection.tsx:234` and `App.tsx:290` callers stay unchanged (default false preserves #142's fix).

Verification via `cargo check` for the Rust side and visual inspection for TS.
verification: `cargo check --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` passes with no new warnings
Grep `load_tiki_releases` callers: existing sidebar + App.tsx callers unchanged (no includeArchived flag, defaults to false)
Grep new call site in useDependencyGraph.ts shows includeArchived: true
Manual: launch desktop app, sidebar ReleasesSection still hides shipped releases (regression check vs #142)
Manual: Dependency Graph dropdown now includes v0.3.0, v0.4.0 (and post-Phase-3 also v0.4.1, v0.4.2, v0.5.0)

phases.title: Backfill v0.4.1, v0.4.2, v0.5.0 release JSONs in archive/
phases.content:
Write three release JSON files to `.tiki/releases/archive/` covering the gap.

For each version (v0.4.1, v0.4.2, v0.5.0):
1. Read the matching `.tiki/releases/v{X}.{Y}.{Z}-changelog.md` for issue references. Most v0.5.0-era releases reference enhancement IDs (E25, E26, etc.) rather than raw issue numbers — cross-reference these to GitHub issue numbers by searching closed issues whose body or title mentions the E-ID.
2. Source the `createdAt` and `completedAt` timestamps from the changelog commit's git log (`git log --format=%aI -1 -- .tiki/releases/v{X}.{Y}.{Z}-changelog.md`).
3. Tag matches version with `v` prefix preserved.
4. Status: `"shipped"` (these are all released).
5. Write file at `.tiki/releases/archive/v{X}.{Y}.{Z}.json` matching the `TikiRelease` Rust struct shape (state.rs:584):
   ```json
   {
     "version": "vX.Y.Z",
     "name": "<theme from changelog>",
     "status": "shipped",
     "issues": [{ "number": N, "title": "..." }, ...],
     "createdAt": "<ISO>",
     "updatedAt": "<ISO ship date>"
   }
   ```
   Extra fields like `completedAt` / `tag` (present in older files like v0.4.0.json) are tolerated by the Rust struct (no deny_unknown_fields), so optional.

Verification: write the three files, then run `node -e 'console.log(require("./.tiki/releases/archive/v0.5.0.json").issues.length)'` and confirm parse + non-empty issue list. Also: load_tiki_releases (post-Phase-2) with includeArchived=true should return them.
verification: ls `.tiki/releases/archive/` shows v0.4.1.json, v0.4.2.json, v0.5.0.json present
Each file is valid JSON parseable as TikiRelease (Rust struct: version, status, issues[], createdAt required)
Issue arrays are populated (non-empty) — sourced from changelog E-ID cross-reference to GitHub closed issues
Manual: launch desktop app post-Phase-2, Dependency Graph dropdown shows v0.5.0, v0.4.2, v0.4.1 entries with correct issue counts

phases.title: Add changelog↔JSON parity check at load time
phases.content:
Add a parity check in the Rust `load_tiki_releases` (or a sibling helper called by it) that:
1. Counts `*-changelog.md` files in top-level `releases/` (and optionally `archive/`).
2. Counts `*.json` files in top-level `releases/` + `archive/`.
3. If changelog count > JSON count, emit `log::warn!('release JSON gap: {N} changelogs without matching JSON: {versions}')` listing the gap versions.

The check runs every time `load_tiki_releases` is invoked. Cost is one `read_dir` per directory — cheap; matches the existing function's already-O(n) cost. No new public API surface; this is internal to commands.rs.

Return the result of `load_tiki_releases` unchanged regardless of the parity result — the warning is informational, not blocking.

Verification: temporarily delete a release JSON and confirm the warn appears in the desktop app's stderr log. Then restore the file.
verification: `cargo check --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` passes
Function returns full release list regardless of parity warn
Manual: rename one archive JSON aside, restart desktop app, confirm warn line in stderr/RUST_LOG output, restore the JSON
Post-Phase-3 with all JSONs present: NO parity warning fires

phases.title: Repair truncated .claude/commands/tiki/audit.md
phases.content:
The `.claude/commands/tiki/audit.md` file is missing roughly 47 lines compared to its mirror at `packages/framework/commands/audit.md`. Specifically the `<errors>` and `<next-actions>` sections were dropped during a previous sync.

Fix: copy the missing tail from `packages/framework/commands/audit.md` (everything after the `</state-management>` closing tag, approximately lines 150-196) into the `.claude/` copy.

Verify via `diff .claude/commands/tiki/audit.md packages/framework/commands/audit.md` — output should be empty (or only show legitimately diverged sections, if any).

This phase is mechanical and independent of the others; it could go in any order. Placed last because it's the cleanest 'finishing touch' that doesn't touch any other code path.
verification: `diff .claude/commands/tiki/audit.md packages/framework/commands/audit.md` produces zero output (files identical)
.claude/ audit.md contains an `<errors>` block and `<next-actions>` block (verify via grep)
Line count of `.claude/` audit.md matches `packages/framework/` audit.md within ±2 lines (allows for trailing newline differences)
